title: File-Delivery Outcomes and Typed Evidence · Inference Brief: Service Level Budget
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing typed file-delivery outcome dossier; use discriminated result unions and exhaustive changed-outcome evaluation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the service level budget increment in typed file-delivery outcome dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set observable availability, latency, integrity, freshness, recovery, accessibility, privacy, and cost targets with measurement windows.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate business file identity, object key, object version, representation bytes, metadata, checksum, URL, and cache entry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Service Level Budget · Theory Service Level Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5958 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set observable availability, latency, integrity, freshness, recovery, accessibility, privacy, and cost targets with measurement windows.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Build Service Level Budget evidence · Theory Service Level Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-service-level-budget-84173w" that uses case 9463 to evaluate a discriminated stakeholder outcome from authorization, custody, accessibility, and cost. Concept boundary: contrast one accepted path, one near miss, and the claim that still needs transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
